Activity: calling home; coordinating visits; sharing updates; supporting each other during illness
Who (real person): Sarah (daughter / healthcare POA of SNF resident)
Frustration / Delight: She feels completely in the dark about her father's daily care. She calls the nursing station 1-2x per day and gets vague updates like 'he had a good day.' She goes to bed anxious and uninformed, and feels guilty she can't visit more. Her siblings across the country are equally disconnected and rely on her secondhand.
Job to be Done: I'm trying to stay informed about my father's daily care so I can feel peace of mind, make good medical decisions, and not feel like a burden every time I call the nursing station.
Value Proposition: We help adult family members of SNF residents stay connected to their loved one's care by translating clinical documentation into plain-English daily updates through a HIPAA-compliant family portal.

Activity: supporting customers; onboarding; training; handling issues
Frustration / Delight: She spends 1-2 hours per shift fielding repetitive phone calls from anxious family members asking questions already documented in the chart — Did she eat? Did the doctor come? Did he sleep okay? — pulling her away from direct patient care and increasing burnout.
Job to be Done: I'm trying to focus on patient care so I can do my job effectively without being constantly interrupted by family calls about information I've already documented.
Value Proposition: We help SNF nursing staff reduce family communication burden by giving families self-serve access to care updates, freeing nurses to focus on patients instead of phones.

Activity: coordinating care decisions; navigating family dynamics; supporting each other during a parent's decline
Who (real person): Toshio (brother and Healthcare POA for SNF resident)
Frustration / Delight: Toshio lives 2 hours away and is responsible for medical decisions. He never knows when the doctor has visited or what was discussed. He finds out days later and feels blindsided, which makes coordinating with the care team and his siblings extremely stressful.
Job to be Done: I'm trying to be kept in the loop on my mother's medical appointments and care decisions so I can fulfill my POA responsibilities and make informed decisions without chasing down information.
Value Proposition: We help healthcare POA family members stay informed on medical visits and care decisions by sending structured, role-based notifications whenever the care team documents significant updates.
